title: IFC Handover for Facility Management: What Building Operators Need
content:
Facility management teams receive IFC models at building handover. What they need from these models is specific: asset registers, maintenance schedules, warranty data, and accurate spatial geometry for space management. Understanding FM requirements shapes the IFC configuration decisions made during design.

Why This Matters

FM teams that receive IFC files without the property sets they need must either manually enter data into their CAFM system or abandon the BIM model. Both outcomes represent a failure of the BIM process and a loss of the investment made during design and construction.

Practical Guidance

Asset Register Data: FM teams need an asset register: every maintainable element with its asset tag, manufacturer, model number, serial number, installation date, expected service life, and warranty expiry. These parameters must be in the IFC as property sets, not in separate spreadsheets.

Spatial Model for Space Management: FM space management tools need accurate room geometry: room number, area, department, floor, and building. The IFC spatial hierarchy (IfcSite, IfcBuilding, IfcBuildingStorey, IfcSpace) must be correctly populated and all rooms must be present in the handover IFC.

Maintenance Zone Data: Group maintainable elements by maintenance zone or access route in the IFC. Property sets that identify maintenance zone, access door number, and service shutdown zone allow FM teams to plan maintenance efficiently without physically inspecting the building first.

CAFM System Test Import: Always perform a test IFC import into the client's CAFM system before formal handover. Test imports reveal property set mapping issues, element classification errors, and missing required fields that can be corrected before handover completion.

Checklist

  • Define FM asset register parameters in BIM Execution Plan at project start
  • Populate asset register data during construction — not in a handover sprint
  • Confirm spatial hierarchy completeness (all rooms present) before handover IFC submission
  • Perform CAFM test import at least 3 months before practical completion

LUA BIM LABS Insight

FM handover IFC success is measured by CAFM import completeness, not by visual geometry quality — configure and test the data flow before handover day.
